finding: security pass: read-bypass gated on exact office id + tool + byte-exact mode==read; spoof-safe; Disabled denied upstream; extraction posture-preserving; no new hole
perms/correctness pass: compute_needs_approval is behaviour-equivalent to the original inline logic for all 6 prior cases; office branch is the only intended delta; pruned dispatch routes correctly
correctness pass: 2 surviving dispatch arms route correctly; all 5 pruned names reach UNKNOWN_TOOL; daemon ignores mode
server_id is Uuid::parse_str'd twice per tool call (once for is_auto_approved, once for compute_needs_approval) — consistent but redundant; parse once and reuse
concurrency pass: compute_needs_approval is pure/sync; run_office_js_read_bypass safe on null/non-object input (get returns None, no panic)

office_bridge_mcp_server_id() recomputes a v5 (SHA-1) hash on every non-control/non-builtin tool call via run_office_js_read_bypass's leading && operand — check tool_name first and cache the id in a LazyLock
copy pass: run_office_js description accurately teaches the read/write mode contract + when-unsure-use-write; no removed-tool names leak; doc updates accurate
api-contract pass: run_office_js inputSchema well-formed (mode enum read|write, required incl mode), consistent with daemon + server input reads

stale seeded_mock doc comment still claims it seeds a PowerPoint doc to exercise the add_comment/set_track_changes PPT capability branch, but that branch + unsupported_on_ppt_err + doc_host + those tests were all removed
naming: server office_bridge_mcp_server_id() (mirrors control_mcp_server_id) vs desktop office_bridge_server_id() (no _mcp_ infix) for a drift-guarded pair
rationale: The server name intentionally mirrors the sibling control_mcp_server_id convention; the desktop office_bridge_server_id predates and is used at several call sites (renaming = churn + out of scope); TEST-11 locks id equality. Adding a cross-reference doc comment instead.

finding: compute_needs_approval takes is_control as a bool even though it is derivable from server_id inside the fn (altitude inconsistency vs is_builtin/is_auto_approved which genuinely need caller precompute)
rationale: The caller already computes is_control for other loop logic; passing it avoids recomputing the control-id v5 hash inside the hot path (deriving it internally would contradict the perf fix). Consistent with how is_builtin (also caller-computed) is passed.

finding: compute_needs_approval unit tests don't cover the ApprovalMode::Disabled=>true fail-safe arm nor the control-WRITE (needs-approval=true) delegation side — only control-read (false) is tested

the load-bearing invariant that office_bridge is NOT in is_builtin_server_id is untested; if it were ever added, compute_needs_approval short-circuits to false and every write auto-runs with no approval, and nothing catches it
retargeted test function name test9_dispatch_tool_read_document_round_trip now dispatches run_office_js — stale/misleading name
